other_observers: solo; Rich Miller in early January 2007 and seen by many others from Jan. to March
specific_location: end of Tunnel Dr. and due west of the parking lot.
description: This was a large plump brownish sparrow with pinkish legs, dull gray breast, GRAYISH BILL, dark eye, and a GOLDEN color on the forecrown only. The remainder of the crown color was a rusty brown in color. The wings were warm brown with two rows of whitish wingbars, the upper wingbar being faint. The tail was gray brown and long. The flanks showed a bit of gray brown as was the lower back and rump. The back was dull brown with darker brown vertical streaking.Behaviors: This bird moved with a flock of about15-20 White-crowned Sparrows, 1st winter birds and adult gambelii. It stayed mostly hidden in skunkbush (Rhus trilobata) as did the majority of the White-crowns. It is possible that it was foraging on the ground under the bushes. I did see it for about 8 seconds teed up on a skunkbush before it moved just into the bush, partly obscured from view.Call: none detectedPlumage: 1st winter
